summary: PR #2661 attempted to make MatToQuat in src/sysdolphin/baselib/quatlib.c match by reducing stack usage. The visible diff removes a 99.9%-match TODO, removes local f32 tr, and reuses local s for the trace expression before sqrtf. Bot output reported one new MatToQuat match from 99.92% to 100.00%, but the PR was closed unmerged and an issue comment says it was superseded by PR #2662.

key_files.role: Only changed file; MatToQuat local variable and trace computation cleanup for matching.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: src/sysdolphin/baselib/quatlib.c added 3 deleted 5 hunks 3
raw_diff: diff --git a/src/sysdolphin/baselib/quatlib.c
human_pr_text: Fix `MatToQuat` in `src/sysdolphin/baselib/quatlib.c` to achieve 100% binary match.

what_changed.text: Removed the comment `/// @todo Currently 99.9% match - stack frame is 8 bytes too large.` above MatToQuat.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: removed TODO line before s32 MatToQuat(Mtx m, Quaternion* q)

what_changed.text: Removed local variable `f32 tr` from MatToQuat.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: removed `f32 tr;`

what_changed.text: Changed the trace expression from assignment to `tr` into assignment to existing local `s`, then tested `s > 0.0F` and used `sqrtf(1.0F + s)`.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: `tr = ...` changed to `s = ...`
raw_diff: `if (tr > 0.0F)` changed to `if (s > 0.0F)`
raw_diff: `s = sqrtf(1.0F + tr)` changed to `s = sqrtf(1.0F + s)`

what_changed.text: PR body claims `int k` was removed by replacing uses with `nxt[j]`, but the loaded diff excerpt still shows `int k;` and contains no visible `nxt[j]` hunk, so this claim is not accepted as confirmed from the diff.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: `int k` was just `nxt[j]` assigned to a temp
raw_diff: declaration block still includes `int k;`

smart_moves.text: Focused the edit on MatToQuat local lifetime/stack behavior rather than broad data or unrelated TU changes.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: one changed file
raw_diff: only MatToQuat hunk shown

smart_moves.text: Reused an existing float local `s` for the trace value before it is overwritten by sqrtf, avoiding an extra stack local in the visible code.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: `f32 tr;` removed and trace assigned to `s`
jurrejelle issue comment: `Re-using a variable is one of the ways to solve this.`

decomp_lessons.lesson: For stack-frame mismatches, removing a local by reusing an existing variable with a non-overlapping lifetime can be a viable matching tactic.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: stack frame was 8 bytes too large
raw_diff: `tr` removed and `s` reused for trace expression
jurrejelle issue comment: `Re-using a variable is one of the ways to solve this.`

decomp_lessons.lesson: Inlining or removing a source-level temporary does not necessarily decrease stack usage if the compiler was already able to inline or optimize that value.
evidence_refs: jurrejelle issue comment: `sometimes stuff that can get inlined already is and thus doesn't actually decrease stack usage`

naming_conventions.text: No new naming convention is established. The PR uses existing short math locals such as `s`, `scale`, `i`, `j`, and `k`; only `tr` is removed in the loaded diff.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: declaration block around `f32 s;`, `f32 scale;`, `int i;`, `int j;`, `int k;`

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Reduce stack-frame size by shortening or merging local-variable lifetimes, specifically by reusing `s` for the trace value instead of keeping a separate `tr` local.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: stack frame was 8 bytes too large
raw_diff: removed `f32 tr;`; assigned trace expression to `s`
decomp-dev[bot] comment: MatToQuat before 99.92%, after 100.00%

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use automated diff/report output to confirm narrow function progress; the loaded bot report says MatToQuat became a new 100.00% match.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl decomp-dev[bot]: `1 new match`; `main/sysdolphin/baselib/quatlib` `MatToQuat` `99.92%` to `100.00%`

review_feedback.author: jurrejelle
review_feedback.text: PR was superseded by PR #2662. Reviewer/commenter noted the author had the right idea, but that values which can be inlined may already be inlined and therefore not reduce stack usage; reusing a variable is one way to solve the stack issue.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl issue_comment by jurrejelle at 2026-06-12T08:54:13Z

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.text: Report for GALE01 showed matched code +0.03%/+1056 bytes and one new match: MatToQuat from 99.92% to 100.00%.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl issue_comment by decomp-dev[bot] at 2026-06-12T07:51:41Z

follow_up_queries: MatToQuat stack frame 8 bytes too large

notes: Loaded diff is complete for the slice and supports the `tr` removal / `s` reuse change.
PR body claims `int k` was removed and replaced with `nxt[j]`, but the loaded diff still shows `int k;` and has no visible hunk proving that change.
Bot report supports a MatToQuat 100.00% result for this PR slice, but the PR was closed unmerged and an issue comment says it was superseded by PR #2662.
The PR body claims the entire quatlib.c unit is now 100% matched across six functions; loaded bot evidence only directly reports one new match for MatToQuat.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Stack-frame fixes may require variable lifetime reuse rather than extra temporaries
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2661, the visible MatToQuat diff removed `f32 tr` and reused `s` for the trace expression before its sqrtf use. A later comment says reusing a variable can solve stack usage issues and that apparent inlining may already be optimized by the compiler.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: `f32 tr;` removed; trace assigned to `s`
text_corpus.jsonl jurrejelle issue_comment: reusing a variable is one way to solve this
text_corpus.jsonl decomp-dev[bot]: MatToQuat 99.92% to 100.00%

source_update_candidates.title: Document variable reuse as a narrow stack-frame matching tactic
source_update_candidates.text: When a stack frame is too large, test whether two locals have non-overlapping lifetimes and whether reusing one source variable reproduces MWCC stack allocation. Do not assume removing an apparent temporary will reduce stack usage if the compiler already optimized it; require objdiff/report evidence.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: MatToQuat removes `f32 tr` and reuses `s`
text_corpus.jsonl jurrejelle issue_comment: `stuff that can get inlined already is` and `Re-using a variable is one of the ways to solve this`
text_corpus.jsonl decomp-dev[bot]: MatToQuat after 100.00%
source_update_candidates.reason: This is a potentially reusable matching tactic, but should be curated into standards only with careful wording because this PR was superseded and the evidence is narrow.

rejection_notes: Do not promote the PR body's `int k` removal as a confirmed changed fact; the loaded diff does not prove it and still shows `int k;`.
Do not promote `quatlib.c` entire-unit 100% status as accepted solely from this PR; bot evidence in the loaded slice directly reports only MatToQuat as the new match, and the PR was superseded.
Do not infer merge acceptance or final project state from this PR; metadata says closed with merged_at null.
